data.title: Sellas Life Sciences files to sell 19.69M shares of common stock for holders
data.description:
• Sellas Life Sciences (SLS) has filed to sell 19,685,040 shares of common stock for holders.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • Filing

data.title: Vince to transfer U.S. stock exchange listing to Nasdaq; shares jump over 12%
data.description:
• Vince (NYSE:VNCE) announced on Friday that it intends to voluntarily transfer its U.S. stock exchange listing to the Nasdaq from the New York Stock Exchange.
  • Vince expects that its common stock will cease listing on the NYSE following market close on or around October 20, 2025, and will commence listing on Nasdaq on or around October 21, 2025.
  • Following the transfer to Nasdaq, the company’s common stock will continue to trade under the symbol “VNCE”.
  • Brendan Hoffman, Chief Executive Officer of VNCE said, “Our transfer to Nasdaq places us alongside other innovative, growth-focused companies. We believe this is an important step as we further our strategic objectives and position the company for long-term success.”
  • VNCE +12.23% after hours to $3.21.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wall Street suffers worst day since April tariff shock
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages plummeted on Friday after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened higher tariffs to China, saying it had "become very hostile" sending letters to other countries wanting to "impose export controls."

Both the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) saw their worst day since April 10, with the former closing -2.7% and the latter ending -3.6%. The Dow (DJI) finished -1.9% -- its worst closing since May 21.

Week-to-date, the S&P lost -2.4%, while the Nasdaq ended -2.5%, and the Dow, -2.7%.

“It is a measure of the number and intensity of late levered longs piling into the market recently that today’s selloff even warrants much mention, let alone the outpouring of grief all over social media. An absolute gift to slow, patient money, particularly if the selldown persists for a couple of weeks. Learn to hedge, people!” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

Tech stocks tumbled after Trump threatened with massive tariffs on China. On the other hand, the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) jumped 32%.

The U.S. entered its tenth day of government shutdown. Prediction market Kalshi is now expecting the shutdown to last more than 31 days.

The University of Michigan's U.S. consumer sentiment survey showed sentiment barely slowing to 55.0, down from 55.1 in September, and better than the 54.1 expected. Also, the 1-year inflation expectation came in at 4.6%, vs. 4.7% consensus.

In addition, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said on Friday that it will publish the September 2025 CPI report by the end of the month to provide the Federal Reserve with inflation data before its next meeting.

Over in the bond market, yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 8 basis points lower at 4.06%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 7 basis points to 3.54%.

Within commodities, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) returned to the $4,000 level as the market selloff attracted investors, while silver (XAGUSD:CUR) surpassed the $50 per ounce level.

Wall Street's major indexes struggled to keep up momentum on Thursday and ended lower, as the U.S. Senate failed to pass two funding bills to end the government shutdown. Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell did not comment on the economic outlook or monetary policy at a Fed event.

data.title: Galaxy announces $460M strategic investment from a leading asset management firm
data.description:
• Galaxy Digital (NASDAQ:GLXY) has entered into investment agreements with one of the world's largest and most respected asset managers for a $460 million private strategic investment in Galaxy's Class A common stock consisting of purchase of 9.02M shares from Galaxy and 3.75M shares from certain executive officers of Galaxy, including its Founder and CEO, at $36 per share.
  • Galaxy intends to use the proceeds from the investment for general corporate purposes and to power the buildout of its Helios data center campus, which remains on schedule to deliver 133 MW of critical IT load in the first half of 2026 as part of its Phase One lease agreement.
  • The investment is expected to close on or about October 17, 2025.
  • BRPHF +0.88% after hours to $39.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tech Voices: China probing Qualcomm, Apple sued over AI, rare earths
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • China’s State Administration of Market Regulation, or SAMR, is reportedly investigating Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) for suspected violations of the country's antitrust laws in its acquisition of Israel's Autotalks, which closed in June.

“We are fully cooperating with SAMR in this matter,” a Qualcomm spokesperson told Barron’s, in a statement. “Qualcomm is committed to supporting the development and growth of our customers and partners.”

  • Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is being sued by two neuroscience professors who allege the company illegally used their copyrighted books to train its Apple Intelligence AI system.

In their proposed class action suit, Professors Susana Martinez-Conde and Stephen Macknik of SUNY Downstate Health Sciences University in Brooklyn, New York, accused Apple of using "shadow libraries" that included two of their books to train the models. The professors are asking the court to award monetary damages and order Apple to stop misusing their copyrighted materials, according to Reuters.

  • Palmer Luckey, the founder of defense technology firm Anduril Industries, said the U.S. needs to get China out of its defense manufacturing supply chain. 

“We have to get off the Chinese supply chain,” Luckey told Bloomberg in an interview following news that China was imposing new export controls on rare earths. “We need to reindustrialize. We need to have our own rare earth supply and make our own chips and computers. China has a lot of leverage.”

data.title: Estée Lauder, e.l.f. Beauty, and Church & Dwight could stand out in an otherwise lackluster quarter - J.P. Morgan
data.description:
The third quarter is shaping up to be another mixed bag for the consumer sector as depressed consumer demand, deleveraging of inventory, and an equally tough environment in Europe likely to result in only a few standouts this earnings season.

Out of the nine stocks covered by J.P. Morgan analyst Andrea Teixeira, only three are expected to positively surprise investors this time around. These include: 

ESTÉE LAUDER (EL): Teixeira looks for Estée Lauder to show sequential improvement in revenue and margins in the fiscal first quarter as “management works towards narrowing the gap between sell-in versus shipments, raising her top-line estimate for FQ1 given that management’s guidance for revenue to be down low-single digits is conservative, especially given easy comparisons from the same quarter last year.

Additionally, Estée Lauder (EL) has lowered its exposure to Asia travel retail -- a segment that has never fully recovered from COVID – to 15% of reported sales from a peak of 29% in FY21.  

The company has increased its online presence, and as such, online sales made up an all-time record of 31% of FY25 sales, a 3-percentage point improvement from FY24 (which includes its debut on Amazon Premium). As a result of this new channel for Estée Lauder, Circana data shows a lift in sales continuing with significant growth in ecommerce channels having a “halo effect” on other channels such as specialty retail and DTC.

Estée Lauder (EL) reports fiscal Q1 results  before the open on Thursday, October 30.

CHURCH & DWIGHT (CHD): “Things are looking up for Church & Dwight amid a conservative outlook,” Teixeira writes.

NielsenIQ tracked channel shows dollar share gains year-over-year for some of Church & Dwight’s (CHD) main categories, including laundry, TheraBreath, and cat litter. Additionally, retailer destocking should begin to alleviate, potentially removing most – if not all – of the lingering 100 basis point headwind left in Q2.

Teixeira expects CHD to report Q3 EPS of $0.74 per share versus $0.72 estimates and organic top-line growth of +2%, 23 basis points better than street estimates.

Church & Dwight (CHD)  reports Q3 results during the trading day on Friday, October 31.

E.L.F. BEAUTY (NYSE:ELF): The company did not provide initial FY26 guidance given the uncertainty with the China tariff rates at the time, but Teixeira expects to see upside from the rhode launch at Sephora with “great upside ahead” despite expectations that sales will likely decelerate from elevated levels at the time of the launch.

“We think ELF will continue to support strong demand as the company recruits more consumers from virtually all income and age cohorts, and market share momentum continues to build based on innovation and repeat purchases,” Teixeira says, adding that ELF’s robust sales growth is proof of strong brand equity.”

e.l.f. Beauty is tentatively expected to  report Q3 results after the close on Monday, November 3.

data.title: AstraZeneca to follow Pfizer lead in making drug price deal with Trump - report
data.description:
• President Trump at 5p ET Friday will say that AstraZeneca has made a deal with the Trump administration to cut the prices of its drugs, following in the footsteps of Pfizer's (PFE) agreement in late September. 
  • The president's daily schedule lists an announcement in the Oval Office at that time.
  • Trump has been pressuring pharma companies to cut prices as part of his "most favored nation," policy, which aims to ensure that Americans pay the same prices for medications as those in other developed countries.
  • MSNBC, which broke the news of the AstraZeneca agreement, said that the company will also invest $50B in manufacturing and R&D in the US.
  • In a July interview with Bloomberg, AstraZeneca CEO Pascal Soriot said that he was open to having American consumers pay drug prices that are similar to that in other wealthy nations. 
  • Pfizer's arrangement with the federal government is to provide heavy discounts on four of its drugs through a new TrumpRx website in return for a three-year break on potential pharma tariffs.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: China drama, big banks report, Oracle and Nvidia events, end-of-life day for Windows 10
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - October 13

  • Volatility watch - Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and USA Rare Earth (USAR) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Churchill Capital Corp. X (CCCX), GigCapital7 (GIG), and Nuburu (BURU). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Stellus Capital (SCM), TaskUs (TASK), and Great Elm Capital (GECC).
  • Politics watch - Investors will have their heads on a swivel to see if the U.S. and China can get closer to a trade deal. Meanwhile, the stalemate between Republicans and Democrats over the government shutdown will roll into its second full week.
  • IPO watch - HW Electro KK (HWEP) is expected to price its IPO and begin to trade during the week. The quiet periods expire on StubHub (STUB), Netskope (NTSK), WaterBridge Infrastructure (WBI), Pattern Group (PTRN), and ChowChow Cloud International (CHOW) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lockup period expires for certain blocks of shares of Chagee Holdings (CHA).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include AbbVie (ABBV), Abbott Labs (ABT), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and Colgate-Palmolive (CL).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Fastenal (FAST).
  • All day - OPEC will issue its monthly oil market report.
  • All day - The Nvidia Corporation Open Compute Project (OCP) Global Summit 2025 will showcase Nvidia's (NVDA) latest innovations in accelerated computing, including AI infrastructure, networking, energy efficiency, and security.
  • All day - The four-day Cloudflare Connect conference will begin. Cloudflare (NET) management will discuss AI, cybersecurity, and cloud infrastructure topics.
  • All day - Oracle (ORCL) will hold its flagship conference, recently retitled Oracle AI World. The event focuses on advancements in cloud, AI, and enterprise technology, featuring high-profile keynote speakers such as Oracle Chief Technology Officer Larry Ellison.
  • 12:10 p.m. Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Anna Paulson will speak before the National Association for Business Economics 67th Annual Meeting.

 

Tuesday - October 14

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Albertsons Companies (ACI), BlackRock (BLK), Citigroup (C), Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Goldman Sachs (GS), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Wells Fargo (WFC), and JPMorgan Chase (JPM).
  • All day - Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will deliver a speech in Philadelphia at the National Association for Business Economics Annual Meeting. The topic will be the "Economic Outlook and Monetary Policy."
  • All day - General Mills (GIS) will hold an Investor Day event. Members of the leadership team will discuss the company's strategic priorities and long-term growth plans.
  • All day - The European Commission extended phase review of the Boeing (BA)-Spirit AeroSystems Holdings (SPR) deal will expire.
  • All day - The launch window opens for Rocket Lab (RKLB) to launch its Electron rocket for Japan-based satellite data and analytics company, Symspective.
  • All day - Microsoft (MSFT) will end Windows 10 support, which could leave hundreds of millions of PCs unprotected. For a fee, Windows 10 users can enroll in one more year of security support. Dell Technologies (DELL), HP Inc. (HPQ), Lenovo (OTCPK:LNVGY), and Best Buy (BBY) have been identified as potential beneficiaries of the laptop and desktop replacement being kicked into a higher gear.
  • 9:00 a.m. The three-day Salesforce Dreamforce 2025 event will begin in San Francisco. Salesforce (CRM) CEO Marc Benioff, Alphabet (GOOG) CEO Sundar Pichai, and Starbucks (SBUX) CEO Brian Niccol will all give talks.
  • 3:30 p.m. Boston Federal Reserve Bank President Susan Collins will speak and participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Greater Boston Chamber of Commerce.

 

Wednesday - October 15

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include ASML (ASML), Bank of America (BAC), Morgan Stanley (MS), Abbott Labs (ABT), United Airlines (UAL), and PNC Financial (PNC).
  • All day - Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.A) (BF.B) will host an investor conference in Nashville, Tennessee. The event will feature a live webcast of presentations by members of the company’s executive leadership team and Jack Daniel’s leaders focused on the Jack Daniel's Family of Brands’ strategic priorities and long-term growth.
  • All day - Dollar Tree (DLTR) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • All day - Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) will hold an analyst meeting to review the company's strategy and long-term financial outlook.
  • All day - Salesforce (CRM) will hold an Investor Day as part of its Dreamforce conference.
  • All day - The NACS Show Expo will begin. The gathering is considered the premier annual event for the convenience and fuel retailing industry. Companies showing off new products include Mars Wrigley, General Mills (GIS), Molson Coors Beverage Company (TAP), Hershey (HSY), and Mondelez International (MDLZ).
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for September is scheduled to be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to hold steady at +3.1%.
  • 1:00 p.m. Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is scheduled to give a speech on AI at the 2025 DC Fintech Week conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.

 

Thursday - October 16

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Charles Schwab (SCHW), U.S. Bancorp (USB), CSX (CSX), and Travelers (TRV). Options trading implies a double-digit share price move for ManpowerGroup (MAN) after it reports.
  • 8:30 a.m. The U.S. Census Bureau is scheduled to release the September Retail Sales report.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.
  • All day - Veeva Systems (VEEV) will host its investor day virtually.
  • All day - Jefferies Financial (JEF) will host its annual Investor Day event in Manhattan.

 

Friday - October 17

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include American Express (AXP), Truist Financial (TFC), and Ally Financial (ALLY).
  • All day - It is the last trading day before the beginning of the blackout period for Federal Reserve Open Market Committee members ahead of the next meeting of the central bank.
  • All day - The three-day TwitchCon event begins. The event holds some relevance for investors, particularly those interested in the digital media, gaming, creator economy, and advertising industries. Amazon (AMZN) purchased Twitch in August 2014 for $970 million.
  • 8:30 a.m. The September Housing Starts report is scheduled to be released by the U.S. Census Bureau and the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: As Trump, Xi trade shots, 'buy tech on sell-off,' Wedbush says
data.description:
Amid the back and forth between President Trump and President Xi Jinping over rare earth metals, Wedbush Securities believes investors should “buy tech” on the sell-off. 

“After a relatively calm few months and improving relations between the US and China, this step up in tensions has created a white-knuckle moment for the markets, with tech stocks under major pressure today,” Wedbush analyst Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients. “As we have navigated this drama since April, we continue to believe the bark will likely be worse than bite this time around as cooler heads prevail. This is all a game of high stakes poker going on between the US and China in this AI Revolution, as we are also seeing more scrutiny in Beijing around Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) golden chips.” 

He continued: “These moments we view as buying opportunities to own the winners in semis, software, Big Tech, and the AI future as in our view these tensions will not bubble up into a much more tense time vs. the nervous period of time we saw in April. That said, our view of tech stocks remains firmly bullish, but we will continue to have moments like this that create some panic and nervousness among tech investors.”

Given that mindset, Ives mentioned the large tech companies, such as Nvidia, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR), Meta (NASDAQ:META), Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) are companies that are benefiting from the AI Industrial Revolution.

“Sell-offs like today we encourage investors to buy the tech winners and not head for elevators despite this war of words between Trump and Xi,” Ives added.

data.title: Semiconductors will grind higher despite U.S.-China tensions – analyst
data.description:
The semiconductor (NASDAQ:SMH), (SOXX), (XSD) ecosystem will continue to grow despite recent trade tensions between the United States and China affecting chipmakers like Qualcomm (QCOM), said Joshua Buchalter, TD Cowen senior analyst.

In an interview by CNBC, the analyst suggested that many of the investigations and export restrictions are being used as negotiating tactics in a larger trade deal that could be announced in the coming months.

“There’s no way to spin it. Increased tech trade tensions between the U.S. and China is not good for the semiconductor index (NASDAQ:SMH), (SOXX), (XSD),” he said.

However, he downplayed concerns about the Qualcomm (QCOM) investigation, noting it relates to “a tuck in acquisition in their auto business” that likely has “very minimal contribution to their revenue today.”

Regarding Qualcomm (QCOM) specifically, Buchalter explained that while the company’s auto business represents about $4B in revenue, the bigger concern would be if restrictions expanded to their smartphone business. “We really think that’s unlikely. Qualcomm (QCOM) is heavily embedded in the smartphone market, and there aren’t obvious and easy replacements for the breadth of their products,” he said.

When discussing AI and valuations in the sector, Buchalter expressed optimism about companies like NVIDIA (NVDA), which he doesn’t consider overvalued despite its nearly $5T market cap.

“If I look at where NVIDIA (NVDA) is trading… at high 20s [multiple], low 30s multiple for the growth rates that they’re putting up and for how important they are to the global compute ecosystem, I actually don’t think it’s over it’s all that expensive,” he said.

The analyst pointed to three simultaneous growth factors driving the semiconductor industry: increasing users, growing complexity of applications and workloads, and expanding use cases for AI.

“I actually think the demand for compute and the gap between supply and demand of where we are today has actually grown over the last twelve months,” he concluded, suggesting that “without these external factors disrupting things, which are impossible for people to predict, I do think this ecosystem is going to continue to grind higher.”
